content.problemStatement:
Your class recently got a maths assignment with 555 questions. There are N(≤20)N (\le 20)N(≤20) students in the class and **at most** 222 people can collaborate. For each student, you know which problems they solved.

Find out if there exists a team of two students who can together solve all problems.


Input Format
The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases. TTT test cases follow.



Each of the following test cases contains N+1N + 1N+1 lines, where NNN is the number of students in the class.

The first line contains a single integer NNN.
Each of the following NNN lines contains Ki+1K_i + 1Ki​+1 positive integers separated by whitespaces.

In the ithi^{th}ith line, the first positive integer KiK_iKi​ is the number of problems the ithi^{th}ith student can solve. The next KiK_iKi​ integers are x1,x2,…,xKix_1, x_2, \ldots, x_{K_i}x1​,x2​,…,xKi​​, the indices of the problems this student can solve.






Output Format
The output must consist of TTT lines.



Each line must contain a single string: The solution to the ithi^{th}ith test case as a `YES` or `NO` (where `YES` should be returned if some pairing of students is capable of solving all the problems, and `NO` otherwise).

You may print each character of the string in uppercase or lowercase (for example, the strings `YES`, `yEs`, `yes`, and `yeS` will all be treated as identical).

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 1:1:1: There is no student who solved the second question.
Test case 2:2:2: The second student can solve the first question and the first student can solve all the remaining problems, so they can form a team to solve all the problems together.
Test case 3:3:3: There is no student who solved fourth and fifth questions.
Test case 4:4:4: Given 222 people can collaborate to solve all the problems.
